dashboard.title: Manage workflows and runs
dashboard.subtitle: Create, publish, run, and handle issues from one place.

authoring.title: Start authoring
authoring.body: Choose a session, update the spec, review results, and publish when checks pass.

catalog.title: View workflows
catalog.body: Browse published versions and inspect each workflow at a glance.

workspace.title: View run details
workspace.body: Track status, inspect node progress, and handle runs that need attention.

settings.title: Adjust system settings
settings.body: Review active settings, connectivity, and runtime defaults.

sidebar.title: Pipeliner
sidebar.subtitle: Workflow Orchestration
sidebar.description: Author workflows, inspect versions, track runs, and handle issues.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: System Settings
settings.description: Review active settings, connectivity, and where each value comes from.

labels.proxyMissingHint: No proxy env detected. Claude CLI may hang or timeout in restricted networks.
labels.claudeTraceHint: Controls whether Claude output logs are retained and displayed.

language.title: Language
language.description: Choose your preferred language for the interface.

authoring.title: Authoring
authoring.subtitle: Update the spec, review results, and publish a version
authoring.description: Use one page to choose a session, edit the spec, and finish publish checks.
authoring.heroDescription: Choose a session, continue editing the spec, review the result, and finish publish checks.
authoring.instructionHint: Describe this round of changes first, then decide whether to save, continue, or let Claude generate the draft directly.
authoring.sessionExplorerHint: Pick an existing session or create a new branch below.
authoring.publishReadinessHint: Publishing only after blocking issues reach zero reduces noisy versions.
authoring.latestDraftHint: This is the spec used by the current session. Edit it directly and the preview will update on the right.
authoring.workflowPreviewHint: Review cards, graph, inputs, and checks here to confirm the impact of your changes.

placeholder.instruction: For example: add input contracts, clarify done_means for each node, and bump the version to 0.2.0.

help.newSession: Create a new session to start a fresh change branch.
help.sessionTitle: Human-readable name used to distinguish this session.
help.intentBrief: A one-line goal that explains the intended outcome of this change.
help.instruction: Describe what should change before you save, continue, or generate.
help.canonicalSpec: This is the spec used by the current session. All derived views come from this JSON.
help.messages: System events, generation logs, and publish activity for this session.
help.revisionHistory: Each save or continue action creates a version you can revisit.

inputs.title: Input definition
inputs.description: Define run inputs here and keep them aligned with the current spec.
inputs.empty: No workflow inputs yet. Add one to define the run-start contract.
inputs.invalidRawSpec: Workflow input controls are unavailable until the raw spec becomes valid JSON.

empty.selectSession: Create or select a session from the left first.
empty.workspaceHint: Select a session to view synchronized cards / graph / spec / lint views.

authoring.sessionEmpty: No authoring sessions yet. Create the first draft track below.
authoring.messageEmpty: No session messages yet. They will appear here after save, continue, or publish actions.
authoring.historyEmpty: No revision history yet. Save or continue once to create one.
authoring.generateReconciling: The generate request failed, but the page is checking whether the backend already produced a new draft.
authoring.generateRecovered: This generate request returned an error, but the backend still produced and synced a new draft successfully.

errors.publishConflict: Version already exists. Please bump the version and retry.

workflows.title: Workflows
workflows.description: Browse published workflows and open a specific version for details.
workflows.selectVersionHint: Choose a version to inspect structure, inputs, and checks.
workflows.runInputsFormHint: Use the structured form generated from workflow input metadata.
workflows.runInputsRawHint: Use raw JSON when you need to bypass or override the structured form.
workflows.runInputsEmpty: This workflow does not declare any inputs. You can still launch the run with an empty payload.
workflows.uploadCsvHint: Each row becomes one run. Runs execute sequentially.
workflows.jsonObjectRequired: Input must be a JSON object
workflows.filePathHint: Manual local path entry is kept plain so the same value can be reused for batch launches.
workflows.iterateHint: Create a new authoring session from this published version.

runs.title: Runs
runs.deleteConfirm: Delete this run and its workspace directory? This cannot be undone.
runs.bulkDeleteRunConfirm: Delete the selected {count} runs and their workspace directories? This cannot be undone.
runs.deleteBatchConfirm: Delete this batch and its related runs and workspaces? This cannot be undone.
runs.bulkDeleteBatchConfirm: Delete the selected {count} batches and their related runs and workspaces? This cannot be undone.
runs.listDescription: Focus first on runs that still need action. Completed runs are tucked into an archive section by default so they do not interrupt active work.
runs.connectionIssueHint: The page cannot confirm the latest state right now. Please retry in a moment.
runs.batchConnectionIssueHint: The page cannot confirm the latest batch state right now. Please retry in a moment.

focus.title: Current Focus
focus.queued: This round has not actually started yet. It is only queued for dispatch, so the terminal will stay empty for now.
focus.empty: No active node round is available right now.

driver.title: Driver state
driver.stalledHint: This run is still marked as running, but auto drive is idle. If no new callbacks or status updates appear for a long time, the run is usually stuck.

graph.title: Run Graph
graph.description: Use the workflow graph as the single source of truth for live node status. Click a node to inspect its latest round.

detail.title: Node Detail
detail.emptyDescription: Select a node in the graph to inspect its run details.
detail.viewingHistorical: You are viewing a non-current node
detail.executorNotStarted: The executor for this round has not started yet. Click "Dispatch now" or trigger auto drive again before terminal output will appear.
detail.executorStartedNoOutput: The executor has started, but it still had no output as of {time}. This usually means Claude is still reading context, waiting on a response, or output is still buffered.
detail.slowStartWarning: The {role} has exceeded the first-byte threshold, but the system will keep waiting. You can continue waiting or stop this run manually.
detail.executorStreaming: The executor has started producing output. This page will keep syncing the latest status automatically.
detail.executorFinishedSyncing: The executor call has finished. The page is syncing the latest run state now.
detail.executorFailedSyncing: The executor call has failed. The page is syncing the failure state now.
detail.waitingValidator: Executor finished. Waiting for the validator to start.

actions.title: Recommended action
actions.retryAndRunHint: Create a new round and continue execution from this node immediately.
actions.continueRunHint: Continue from the current waiting step.
actions.startExecutionHint: This round is ready. Executor output will appear after it actually starts.
actions.startValidationHint: This run is waiting on a validator step that you can continue here.
actions.returnToLatestHint: You are viewing a historical round. Go back to the latest round before taking action.
actions.runningHint: Auto drive is already progressing this run. No manual action is needed right now.
actions.stoppedHint: This run has already been stopped. Start a new run if you need to continue.
actions.noneHint: There is nothing that needs immediate action right now.

groups.batchHint: Batch launches stay visible here so you can re-enter the batch detail view later.
groups.actionableHint: These runs need you to continue, retry, or confirm something.
groups.activeHint: These runs are still moving and usually do not need immediate intervention.
groups.archivedHint: Historical runs stay collapsed by default until you need them.

empty.batch: There are no batch runs yet.
empty.actionable: There are no runs that need action right now.
empty.active: There are no runs in progress right now.
empty.archived: There are no archived runs yet.

listSummary.running: This run is still progressing. Check back for the latest updates.
listSummary.completed: This run finished successfully. Open it to review results and artifacts.
listSummary.stopped: This run has been stopped. Open it to review the stop reason.
listSummary.default: Open the run to inspect its current state and next action.

stopReasons.noDispatchableNodes: There are no nodes that can continue automatically.
stopReasons.terminalState: The run reached its current terminal state.
stopReasons.maxStepsExceeded: Auto drive stopped after reaching the step limit.
stopReasons.timeoutGuardExceeded: The system waited too long for this round to report progress. This may mean the task never started correctly, or that the result never made it back. Check the call state and logs first.
stopReasons.claudeProcessExited: The execution process exited unexpectedly before a result was collected. This is closer to an execution failure than a task simply taking too long.
stopReasons.claudeCallStale: The execution call stopped reporting any result for too long, so the system marked it as stale. This is closer to an execution failure than a slow task.
stopReasons.reworkLimit: The maximum rework rounds were reached, so the system stopped retrying.
stopReasons.executorFirstByteTimeout: The executor started, but produced no output before the first-byte timeout. This usually means the model call hung, the connection stalled, or the task is too heavy to return an initial response quickly.
stopReasons.validatorFirstByteTimeout: The validator started, but produced no output before the first-byte timeout. This usually means the model call hung, the connection stalled, or the task is too heavy to return an initial response quickly.
stopReasons.validatorBlocked: Validation did not pass. The run is paused for manual attention.
stopReasons.validatorRequestedRevise: Validation requested a revision. Re-run this node after applying the feedback.
stopReasons.executorTimeout: The executor itself ran for too long and timed out. This usually means the task is genuinely slow, so increasing the timeout or splitting the task may help.
stopReasons.validatorTimeout: The validator itself ran for too long and timed out. This usually means the task is genuinely slow, so increasing the timeout or splitting the task may help.
stopReasons.executorFailed: The executor failed. Check this round's logs.
stopReasons.validatorFailed: The validator failed. Check this round's logs.
stopReasons.executorCommandFailed: The executor command failed. Check the terminal logs and command configuration.
stopReasons.validatorCommandFailed: The validator command failed. Check the terminal logs and command configuration.
stopReasons.driverStaleRecovered: Auto drive had already stopped. The previous node result was recovered, but later nodes did not continue. Retry from the current failed node.
stopReasons.driverFailed: Auto drive failed and the run is now paused. Technical detail: {detail}
stopReasons.runStateUnexpected: The run entered an unexpected state while progressing: {detail}

runs.noArtifactsInRound: No artifacts published in this round.
runs.noLogsInRound: No logs recorded for this round.
runs.callbacksEmpty: No callbacks recorded for this round.

drive.title: Auto drive
drive.description: Dispatch nodes in order until terminal state or max steps.
drive.runningDescription: Auto-drive is already running for this run.
drive.invalidSteps: Max steps must be a positive integer
drive.disabledWhileRunning: Manual drive is disabled while another driver is running.

preview.title: Artifact / Log Preview
preview.truncated: Preview truncated at {limit} bytes. Use the workspace path to open the full file.

runs.noAttentionNodes: No attention nodes at the moment.

batchRuns.title: Batch Runs
batchRuns.empty: No rows found in this batch.
batchRuns.autoRefresh: Auto-refreshing while this batch is still active.

workflow.graphEmpty: No graph nodes are available for this version. Check nodes in the workflow spec.

attention.title: Needs Action
attention.iterateHint: Start a new authoring session from this run to fix or refine the workflow.
